title: AMTTP Transaction Dispute
description: Dispute resolution for AMTTP (Anti-Money Transfer Transaction Protocol) escrow transactions. This protocol enables secure P2P cryptocurrency transfers with built-in KYC/AML compliance and fraud detection.
question: Should the escrowed funds be released to the intended recipient?

descriptions: The jurors find that the transaction is legitimate and the funds should be released to the recipient as intended.
The jurors find evidence of fraud, policy violation, or other issues. The escrowed funds should be returned to the sender.

required.name: Transaction Details
required.description: On-chain transaction data including sender, receiver, amount, and timestamp

required.name: Risk Assessment
required.description: ML model risk score and contributing factors

optional.name: KYC Documentation
optional.description: Identity verification status and any relevant compliance data (redacted as needed)

optional.name: Supporting Documents
optional.description: Invoices, contracts, or other documentation supporting the transaction

disputeGuidelines: - Both parties have verified KYC status
- Sender claims funds were sent in error
- Pattern of rapid transactions to new wallets
- ML risk scores are advisory, not deterministic
- KYC status is a strong indicator
- If insufficient evidence from both parties, favor release to recipient
- The challenger (sender or third party) bears burden of proof
